skill.name: vector-text-fixer
skill.description: Fix garbled text in PDF/SVG vector graphics caused by font encoding issues, making files editable in AI tools. Supports batch processing and JSON export for manual correction.
skill.summary: Repairs garbled text in PDF and SVG vector graphics caused by font encoding and embedding errors.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: All 77 static analysis findings are false positives after manual evaluation. Hex-encoded characters and unicode escape sequences are legitimate garbled-text detection patterns in scripts/main.py for identifying replacement characters and encoding errors. Shell execution alerts correspond to markdown documentation code blocks in SKILL.md showing CLI usage examples, not runtime shell execution. Cryptographic algorithm alerts are argparse description strings and unrelated references in documentation. Network alerts are a GitHub source URL reference, not runtime network access. The skill is a legitimate file processing tool with no malicious behavior.

content.user_title: Fix Garbled Text in PDF and SVG Vector Graphics
content.value_statement: Vector text in PDF and SVG files often becomes unreadable due to font encoding errors and embedding problems. This tool detects garbled characters, identifies the encoding issue, and provides suggested fixes or exports editable JSON for manual correction.
actual_capabilities: Detect garbled text in PDF files using character analysis and pattern matching against known replacement characters and control sequences
Fix broken text encoding in SVG files including XML entity issues and mixed encoding signatures
Batch process multiple PDF and SVG files in a folder with a single command
Export text repair data as editable JSON for manual review and correction in AI tools
Support three repair levels: minimal for obvious errors, standard for common issues, and aggressive for severely garbled text
Identify mixed encoding issues such as UTF-8 bytes misread as Latin-1 characters
limitations: Cannot process encrypted PDF files without password unlock before processing
Severely damaged vector files may not be fully repairable and require manual intervention
Rare or unusual fonts may not map correctly during the repair process
Scanned PDFs require OCR recognition first before garbled text repair can be applied

use_cases.title: Fix unreadable PDF text for academic research
use_cases.description: A researcher has a PDF with box characters or question marks instead of readable text due to font embedding problems. The tool scans the document, identifies garbled blocks, and restores readable text.

use_cases.title: Repair SVG text for graphic design workflow
use_cases.description: A graphic designer receives SVG files with garbled text from encoding mismatches. The tool batch processes the files and produces clean, editable text for use in vector editing software.

use_cases.title: Export text for manual correction in AI tools
use_cases.description: A data analyst has PDF reports with mixed encoding across multiple languages. The tool exports a structured JSON file with each garbled block and its suggested fix for human review.

prompt_templates.title: Fix a single garbled PDF file
prompt_templates.prompt: Please fix the garbled text in this PDF file. The file is located at path/to/document.pdf. Use standard repair level and save the output to path/to/fixed.pdf.
prompt_templates.scenario: User has one PDF with garbled text characters from font encoding issues

prompt_templates.title: Batch process multiple SVG files
prompt_templates.prompt: Batch repair all garbled SVG files in the folder path/to/svgs/. Output the fixed files to path/to/fixed_svgs/. Use the standard repair level.
prompt_templates.scenario: User needs to fix encoding problems in multiple SVG files at once

prompt_templates.title: Export editable JSON for manual review
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze the PDF at path/to/document.pdf for garbled text and export the repair results as JSON to path/to/editable.json. I want to review the suggested fixes manually before applying them.
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants to verify and manually correct each garbled text block

prompt_templates.title: Aggressive repair with mixed encoding
prompt_templates.prompt: The file path/to/mixed_encoding.pdf has garbled text from multiple encoding issues including UTF-8 misread as Latin-1. Use aggressive repair level to try all available decoding strategies. Save to path/to/fixed.pdf.
prompt_templates.scenario: User has a PDF with complex multi-encoding garbled text that needs deep repair

output_examples.input: I have a PDF scientific paper where all the text shows as boxes and question marks.
output_examples.output: The tool scanned the PDF and found 23 garbled text blocks out of 156 total blocks. Garbled text was primarily caused by missing font substitution. 18 blocks were repaired automatically and 5 low-confidence blocks were flagged for manual review.

output_examples.input: My SVG icons exported from a legacy tool have strange characters instead of labels.
output_examples.output: The tool processed 12 SVG files in the batch folder. 8 files had garbled text elements. XML entity encoding errors were detected and corrected. A total of 47 garbled text elements were identified and repaired.

best_practices: Always review low-confidence repair results manually before using the fixed files in production.
Use the standard repair level for most cases and only use aggressive repair when text is severely garbled.
For encrypted PDFs, unlock the file first using a PDF password removal tool before processing.
anti_patterns: Do not use this tool for OCR or scanned document text extraction. Use a dedicated OCR tool first.
Do not use aggressive repair level on files with only minor encoding issues as it may introduce unnecessary changes.
Do not rely on automated fixes for critical documents without human verification of the output quality.

faq.question: What types of garbled text can this tool fix?
faq.answer: It fixes text damaged by font embedding problems, encoding conversion errors, missing font substitution characters, and multi-language mixed encoding issues in PDF and SVG files.

faq.question: Does this tool perform OCR on scanned documents?
faq.answer: No. This tool only repairs garbled text in vector-based PDF and SVG files. Scanned documents require OCR processing before using this tool.

faq.question: What are the three repair levels and when should I use each?
faq.answer: Minimal removes only obvious error characters. Standard handles common encoding issues with smart font replacement. Aggressive applies full re-encoding for severely garbled documents.

faq.question: Can this tool process multiple files at once?
faq.answer: Yes. Use the batch mode with the --batch flag to process all PDF and SVG files in a folder automatically.

faq.question: How does the tool detect garbled text?
faq.answer: It analyzes replacement characters such as boxes and question marks, control characters, abnormal character distribution, and mixed encoding signatures to identify garbled text.

faq.question: Can I review and manually correct the suggested fixes?
faq.answer: Yes. Use the --export-json flag to export the repair data as a structured JSON file with editable fields for manual correction in AI tools.
